title: /kernel:run
summary_text:
- `kernel-lane-bridge.mjs` is the live probe/orchestrator source. It writes lane evidence to `~/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/state/active-model-lanes.jsonl` and `lane-runs.jsonl` (set by `KERNEL_ACTIVE_MODEL_LOG` / `KERNEL_LANE_RUNS_LOG`; defaults are in that file). - `collectStatus()` in the bridge computes provider health from runtime availability, not from JSONL logs. Health checks: - executable presence (`commandExists`, script file presence, token/env checks), e.g. `codex` CLI, `gemini-cli` command, `ZAI_API_KEY`, `GH_TOKEN`, `XAI_API_KEY`, etc. - returns `{provider: {available, reason}}`; `--check` prints this snapshot. - The bridge logs each probe/run via `appendActiveModelRecord(...)` to `active-model-lanes.jsonl` only when a lane is executed, with fields like `requestedProvider/provider/role/model/ok`. - `--provider

` execution also logs a run outcome to `lane-runs.jsonl` (plus parsed output), so historical failures/successes are there too, but the “active model” criterion for guard is driven by the active-model file stream. - For guard-side gating, `recent_active_models_json(reference_time)` reads only `active-model-lanes.jsonl` and selects rows in a 60‑minute window ending at completion time; it keeps only last 12 qualifying records as evidence. - `has_required_external_models(...)` in `task_backup.py` requires: - `provider == "glm"` observed with `ok: true` - at least one specialist in `REQUIRED_SPECIALIST_PROVIDERS` observed with `ok: true` (currently `("gemini-cli", "copilot")`) - In kernel launch flow (`cli.py`), observed models for the launch are: - `recent_active_models_json(...)` by default, or - `_observed_models_from_provider_reprobe(...)` when reprobe is enabled and bootstrap evidence exists, which directly probes glm + one specialist provider live. - `kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh` only stores receipt metadata; it can include `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV` / label flags but does not itself derive evidence.
